checks.name: existing-env-confirmation
checks.detail: Interactive install reads the selected repo .env, shows an Existing .env Values confirmation screen before Telegram setup when an existing token is present, and masks the bot token.

checks.name: safe-preview-values
checks.detail: The confirmation screen displays safe non-secret existing values plainly, including fake allowed user IDs, home channel, bot username, local URLs, and port overrides.

checks.name: edit-path-blank-form
checks.detail: Choosing edit opens Telegram setup with a blank token and without allowed user IDs or home channel from .env or saved draft state.

checks.name: reuse-path-carries-values
checks.detail: Choosing reuse carries existing Telegram token, allowed user IDs, and home channel into the install result and env merge.

checks.name: non-interactive-and-cli-compatibility
checks.detail: Non-interactive fallback still uses CLI, draft, then existing .env values; CLI --allowed-user remains operator-provided input in interactive mode.

checks.name: fresh-verification-commands
checks.detail: Fresh verifier reruns passed: targeted install unit tests, targeted install runtime tests, pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap build, pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap lint, and pnpm happytg task validate.

checks.name: scope-boundary
checks.detail: Current workspace changes include out-of-scope Docker/launch/port/docs/policy/runtime surfaces, so the task cannot pass as an isolated implementation of the frozen spec.

findings.detail: The frozen spec excludes unrelated installer launch, Docker, port-preflight, doctor, and verify behavior, but the current workspace diff includes those surfaces and additional unrelated package changes.
